spec_match.rationale: The demo is a pure JS simulation. It falsely detects <selectedcontent> support using customElements.get('selectedcontent') (which will always return false since it's a native tag, not a custom element), and uses synchronous DOM text/style updates under the hood.

interactive.rationale: Allows selecting a color with immediate simultaneous updates to three mirror cards (toolbar badge, status bar, and breadcrumb) with a live timestamped history log.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the Chrome 150 multiple mirrors cloning specification.

edge_cases.rationale: Attaches event listeners to update the divs but does not actually exercise or test native <selectedcontent> tags.

references.rationale: Links back to feature index and ChromeStatus entry.

design_system.rationale: Beautiful card layout, color swatches, high contrast, and highly readable log blocks.

openQuestions.title: Fix native support detection
openQuestions.detail: Checking customElements.get('selectedcontent') is incorrect. It should check `typeof HTMLSelectedContentElement !== 'undefined'` or inspect `document.createElement('selectedcontent').constructor`.

openQuestions.title: Use real <selectedcontent> elements
openQuestions.detail: Instead of simulating the mirrors via raw divs and event listeners, the demo should render real <selectedcontent> elements inside a customizable select structure when supported natively.

summary: A highly polished simulator for multiple selectedcontent mirrors, though it relies on incorrect support-detection logic and simulates behavior purely in JS without native tags.
